_meta.description: 2026-07-18 Q&A-engine rebuild: agents now ask real questions directly to Claude (data-center) or Gemini (a specific Notebook-X notebook) — see CLAUDE.md and workers/qa-engine.js. The old per-day CALL-COUNT Claude cap (claude_daily_cap, removed below) is retired in favor of a single shared DOLLAR budget (shared_claude_budget below) tracked in D1's claude_budget_usage table (workers/model-router.js) — the SAME table the chore-automation economy already used, not a new separate one. Routine persona flavor (coworker chat, follow-up phrasing, AI-experience notes, critiques) runs Groq-first with Cloudflare fallback via agent-base.js queryGroqRouted() (renamed 2026-07-19 from the misleadingly-named queryGemini()); Hebrew/bilingual composition (gap notes, Trainee guides) goes direct to Gemini via queryGeminiDirect(), meeting-engine report synthesis calls callGemini() itself, and Notebook-X asks also go direct to Gemini, paced by workers/gemini-pacer.js. Agent 10 (Architect) is dormant — excluded from this flow entirely, not just from routine cases.

shared_claude_budget._meta: Added 2026-07-18, replaces the retired claude_daily_cap (a per-day CALL COUNT, checked against `interactions` rows). This is a per-MONTH DOLLAR cap, checked/recorded via workers/model-router.js's getClaudeBudgetStatus()/recordClaudeSpend() — the exact same D1 claude_budget_usage table and functions the chore-automation economy already used (see chore_automation.claude_budget_usd_per_month below, now the single source of truth for this cap; briefly bumped 4.50 -> 5.00 on 2026-07-18 when the budgets were unified, then restored to 4.50 later that day as a deliberate soft-stop leaving real headroom under the account's own $5/month spend ceiling — the two numbers are distinct mechanisms, software soft-stop vs account hard limit). 'Shared' is not aspirational — office-agent Q&A Claude calls (agents/agent-base.js _askDataCenter()) and chore-automation Claude calls (workers/chore-runner.js) draw from and record against the SAME month row, by design, per this session's explicit instruction not to create a second budget.
shared_claude_budget._max_calls_per_day_meta: Added 2026-07-19 (owner-approved rebalance): hard per-day Claude CALL cap — calls, not questions, so follow-up asks count too. Enforced at BOTH layers: (a) generation time — workers/qa-engine.js generateAssignedDailyBatch() assigns at most this many data-center-targeted questions per generation call (one call per simulated day), overflow re-picked from the notebook-x pool; (b) ask time — agents/agent-base.js _askDataCenter() skips (logged, tool_used 'claude-daily-cap-skip') once today's interactions rows with model_source='claude' reach this count (model-router.js getClaudeCallsToday(), UTC calendar day per this repo's DATE('now') convention — a simulated Israel day spans two UTC dates at the 23:00Z first batch, so the effective worst case is ~cap+first-batch, still well under the monthly soft-stop). Worst case 10 x ~$0.0101 x 31d ~= $3.13/mo vs the $4.50 soft-stop. NOT the same mechanism as the retired claude_daily_cap (30, which gated ALL agent Claude usage before the dollar budget existed); this is a routing-mix control layered UNDER the monthly dollar cap, not a replacement for it.

graduated_rollout_throttle._meta: Added 2026-07-18 for the FIRST LIVE ACTIVATION of the Q&A engine (cron re-enabled the same day, first run 2026-07-19). This is a TEMPORARY graduated-rollout throttle, NOT a permanent volume limit: workers/agent-runner.js's computeDailyQuestionVolume() caps the day's generated question total at ramp_daily_caps[N], where N = calendar days elapsed in Israel time since activation_date_israel (N=0 on the activation day itself). Once N >= ramp_daily_caps.length the throttle is automatically inert — volume returns to the normal budget-driven computation with NO manual step-up needed. The per-call $-budget check (_askDataCenter) and Gemini pacing (gemini-pacer.js) remain the real backstops throughout. Safe to delete this whole block once the ramp window has passed; applyGraduatedRolloutCap() treats a missing block as 'no throttle'.

notebook_x_gemini_pacing._meta: Added 2026-07-18. See workers/gemini-pacer.js's header comment for the full reasoning — summary here for anyone reading this config without the code.
notebook_x_gemini_pacing.why_not_a_precise_quota_split: Gemini's free-tier per-minute quota is shared across THREE consumers this repo cannot fully observe: Notebook-X's own backend (live /ask traffic + its weekly gap-analysis job), this office Q&A automation, and other existing Gemini usage in office-AI-agents itself (report synthesis, coworker chat, gap-report composition). With two of the three consumers invisible to this repo, computing an exact 'fair share' would be false precision — the only safe posture is conservative, wide spacing from THIS automation, leaving deliberate headroom rather than trying to use a computed maximum.
notebook_x_gemini_pacing.mechanism: skip-if-too-soon via a KV-tracked last-call timestamp, not an in-Worker blocking sleep — see checkGeminiPacingSlot().

description: Groq and CF AI are free — run agents hard. Claude is paid — capped by shared_claude_budget above ($4.50/month soft-stop shared with chore-automation, under the account's own $5/month spend ceiling), not a per-day call count. Primary purpose: real usage of both production AI systems (Claude, Gemini) surfaces genuine capability gaps.

_meta.description: Routing for the TODO.md-driven chore/content-generation automation (config/daily-schedule.json's chore_rotation block, workers/chore-runner.js/model-router.js) — a real feature, separate from the 11-agent Q&A office simulation's own daily flow (workers/qa-engine.js). NOTE: TODO.md itself was deleted from this repo's root in the 2026-07-18 repo-cleanup session; workers/chore-runner.js's fetchTodoSection() fetches it via a raw GitHub URL and degrades to a permanent no-op (ranTask:false, 'no tasks configured') now that it 404s — not a crash, but effectively dormant until TODO.md exists again or this is rewired to read something else.
_meta.relationship_to_case_sim_cap: UPDATED 2026-07-18 — this is now the SAME budget as shared_claude_budget above (top-level), not a separate one. claude_budget_usd_per_month below is the single source of truth both blocks point to; office-agent Q&A Claude calls (agent-base.js) and chore-automation Claude calls both read/write the same D1 claude_budget_usage month row via workers/model-router.js's getClaudeBudgetStatus()/recordClaudeSpend(). The old 11-agent-only per-day CALL-COUNT cap (claude_daily_cap) this note used to contrast against no longer exists.

chore_automation.claude_scope: code-writing tasks and approval/review decisions only, by default. Notebook-X overrides this scope — see notebook_x_override below.
chore_automation.claude_pricing_note: Estimated at current Sonnet 5 intro pricing: $2/M input tokens, $10/M output tokens (workers/model-router.js CLAUDE_PRICING). This changes to $3/M input / $15/M output after 2026-08-31 — model-router.js already branches on that date, but double check the real published price at that time before trusting the estimate.
chore_automation.gemini_role: expanded — default writer for routine content generation across chore-automation tasks (previously reserved for report synthesis only in the office-simulation economy above).
chore_automation.groq_role: routine/easy sub-tasks only (simple formatting, short lookups, classification) — not full content generation.

notebook_x_override.applies_to: notebook-x project only — other projects (data-center, archive-alpha) use the general chore_automation economy above, unchanged.
notebook_x_override.claude_when: task complexity/quality genuinely demands it. Still drawn from the SAME $4.50/mo claude_budget_usd_per_month cap above — this override does not add a second budget.
notebook_x_override.groq_scope: clearly 'easy' sub-tasks only within Notebook-X work (e.g. simple formatting, short lookups) — never content generation itself.
